title: Filter
description: This is a double array representing filters combined with AND and OR operators. The first level is combined with an AND operator and the second one with an OR. For example: The JSON [[{"field":"name","type":"==","value":"doe"},{"field":"age","type":"<=","value":42}],[{"field":"address.country","type":"regexp","value":"^EN$|^FR$"}]] is equivalent to the boolean logic ((name==doe OR age<=42) AND (address.country match "EN" or "FR")).

items.title: Rule
items.description: Represents a single rule that can be combined into a filter.

field.title: Field selector
field.description: The path used to get the field of an input item to evaluate against. It can target a nested field, in which case it's dot-separated. The empty string selects the whole element (e.g. to filter a list of scalars); the key itself is required.

type.title: The type of the rule
type.description: The operator to apply. Matched case-insensitively, so "regexp", "REGEXP" and "==" are all valid; the enumerated forms below are the canonical lowercase spellings.

value.title: The value to evaluate against
value.description: The reference value. The key is required; use null for a nil reference (e.g. with == to match a nil field). Arrays and objects are not accepted.
